page_content:
ANSC497 Animal Biotechnology Recombinant DNA Laboratory (3 Credits) An advanced course offering hands-on experience in performing recombinant DNA experiments. Current molecular biology techniques used for cloning genes, analyzing the gene products, and modifying the genes of animals will be performed. Techniques include isolation of DNA, use of restriction enzymes; cloning procedures, PCR analysis, and Southern hybridizations. Lecture material focuses on interpretation of results generated in the laboratory. Prerequisite: ANSC327; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department. Recommended: ANSC437 and ANSC435.

ANSC617 Quantative Techniques in Physiology and Nutrition (3 Credits) Development and evaluation of quantative techniques to explore mechanisims of physiological and nutritional regulation. Kinetic and dynamic models will be emphasized. Prerequisite: MATH120; or permission of AGNR-Animal & Avian Sciences department.
